description: Canonical release-result manifest (#298). Every headline claim in README.md and MODEL_CARD.md derives from this file; tests/unit/test_publication_claims.py fails CI when a publication surface or this manifest drifts from .audit/baseline_metrics.json.

release.fit_produced_on: 0.12.x (bit-identical to the flipped 0.13.0 default via the likelihood-registry parity lock)

identity.preset: publication (amended for 0.13.0): 4 chains x 5000 draws, warmup 5000, target_accept 0.90

raw_corpus.note: all_albums_full.csv scrape; includes albums without user scores

eligible_corpus.note: albums with a user score and >= 10 user ratings — the population the validated subset was sampled from and the full-corpus target of #15

validated_subset.note: whole artists sampled with full discographies (scripts/make_aoty_subset.py)

evaluated_test.note: multi-album artists with >= 10 ratings; one held-out next album per artist on the primary split

ppc.note: q10 sits near the 0.025 boundary but is interior; the entity-obs default cleared q10 and q90, the first movement on the bounded-skew tail misfit. skewness and max remain the confirmed structural mismatch.

limitations: skewness/max PPC pins are a confirmed structural symmetric-likelihood vs left-skewed-target mismatch; six alternative likelihood families plus dequantization did not resolve them (docs/LIKELIHOOD_CANDIDATES.md)
all numbers are from the ~5k-album validated subset, not the full eligible corpus; full-corpus validation is tracked as #15 and needs >24 GB GPU
domain portability is structural, not predictive: accuracy off the AOTY flagship is untested by construction
